meaning: adj.精神错乱的,疯狂的,极端愚蠢的n.疯子,狂人,大傻瓜,疯人
examples: This lunatic in a white van pulled out right in front of me!
As for her personal safety, she said she isn't overly worried but does think about what a stray lunatic could do.
He was considered a "lunatic" by fellow townspeople and was known as a loner.
There were other lunatic moments in Mr Plimpton's experiences in what he called participation.
